name: Discord Node MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Discord enabling AI agents to interact with Discord servers — sending messages, reading channel history, managing members and roles, creating channels, and responding to events. Built on discord.js (Node.js), supporting full Discord bot capabilities through the MCP protocol.
use_cases: Sending messages and announcements to Discord channels from AI notification agents
Monitoring Discord channels for mentions and queries from community management agents
Managing Discord server members, roles, and permissions from moderation agents
Building AI-driven Discord bots that respond to events and commands
not_for: Slack, Teams, or other communication platforms (Discord-specific)
Teams without Discord servers or bots configured
Production Discord bots without proper rate limit handling
best_when: A community manager or developer with a Discord server wants AI agents to send announcements, moderate content, and respond to community events automatically.
avoid_when: You use Slack, Teams, or other platforms. Also: Discord bot requires server admin setup and bot token management.

auth.notes: Discord Bot Token required. Bot must be added to Discord server with appropriate permissions. Discord Developer Portal registration needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Discord API is free for bot usage within rate limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Discord Nitro for users; bot API remains free
pricing.notes: Discord bot API is free. MCP server is free open source (Node.js/discord.js).

performance.rate_limits: Discord API: 50 requests/second globally, per-route limits apply

known_agent_gotchas: Discord requires Bot Token (not OAuth user token) — register bot on Discord Developer Portal first
Rate limits are per-route — bulk messaging can trigger 429 errors; implement exponential backoff
Bot must be in the target server with correct permissions — intents must be enabled on portal
Message history access requires MESSAGE_CONTENT intent (privileged) — must be approved by Discord for large bots
agent_readiness.security_notes: Bot token grants server-level access — store securely. Limit bot permissions to minimum required. Discord data includes user messages — handle per your privacy policy.

security_score_components.security_notes: Bot token security critical. Least-privilege permissions. User message data sensitivity.
